summary: Resolved the app repo attachment dirtiness by ignoring .codex-remote-attachments, added a first-class task:finish:stdio fallback for closed MCP transports, and prepared the remaining DinoBrain data backlog for a final safety-checked commit.
decisions: Treat .codex-remote-attachments as local Codex UI artifacts and ignore them instead of committing screenshots and remote attachments.
Use a fresh stdio MCP finish helper when the current Codex MCP transport is closed, so task closure is still recorded through the official finish_task tool.
Stage the full policy-approved DinoBrain data backlog after finish_task so the data repo can return to a clean state without dropping generated OS records.
next_steps: Commit and push the staged DinoBrain data backlog after safety hooks pass.
Use task:finish:stdio whenever the in-session mcp__dinobrain transport is closed.
